title: Malache subpandurata
intro: Malache subpandurata, also known as Pando Malabar or Malabar Pando, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Malache subpandurata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic and Siddha medicine, Malache subpandurata is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, and is used to make various herbal preparations, including capsules, tablets, and topical creams, to treat a range of health conditions.
